title: 10 best herbal creams for migraine

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asafoetida creams helps with migraine because they contain a natural compound that relaxes blood vessels and reduces inflammation, which are common contributors to migraines. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help alleviate pressure and tension in the head and neck, while its vasodilatory effects ease blood flow and reduce pain. Additionally, asafoetida's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate migraine symptoms. By targeting multiple underlying causes of migraines, asafoetida creams provide effective relief from debilitating headaches and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Asafoetida cream's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and pain associated with migraine, providing relief from headache and discomfort.
Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic property of asafoetida cream helps to relax the blood vessels and muscles in the neck and head, reducing spasms that can contribute to migraine attacks.
Analgesic property: Asafoetida cream's analgesic property provides pain relief by blocking pain signals to the brain, helping to reduce headache severity and duration.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the asafoetida plant contain a high concentration of sulfur compounds, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving migraine pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of asafoetida have been traditionally used to treat headaches, including migraines, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and relax the blood vessels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried ferula foetida roots and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered ferula foetida with 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat it gently at 60°c for 30 minutes.
Add 5 grams of beeswax to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated, then continue heating for an additional 15 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to room temperature, allowing it to solidify completely before transferring it to a storage container.
Package the finished cream in airtight containers and label them with expiration dates and ingredients, storing them in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with migraine because they provide a soothing and calming effect on the skin. The gel-like substance in aloe contains anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and relax tense muscles, which can contribute to migraines. Additionally, aloe's natural cooling sensation can help ease tension headaches by reducing inflammation and promoting blood flow to the affected area. By applying an herbal aloe cream topically, individuals may find relief from migraine symptoms such as pain, sensitivity, and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera contains anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin, aloe-emodin, and other anthraquinones that help reduce inflammation in the brain and scalp, which can contribute to migraines.

Antioxidants: Herbal aloe creams contain antioxidants like vitamins C and E, beta-carotene, and other polyphenols that help neutralize free radicals and oxidative stress in the body, which can trigger migraine attacks.

Vasoactive compounds: Aloe vera contains vasoactive compounds like saponins and glycoproteins that help improve blood flow, relax blood vessels, and reduce vasodilation, which can contribute to migraines.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves contain anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help reduce pain and inflammation associated with migraines.
Stems: The stems of the aloe plant contain a compound called cinnamic acid, which has analgesic properties that can help alleviate migraine headaches.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one cup of aloe vera gel and two tablespoons of coconut oil in a mixing bowl.
Combine three teaspoons of dried lavender flowers and one teaspoon of chamomile flowers in the mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the oils have melted and the herbs are fragrant.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to room temperature before whipping with an electric mixer for five minutes.
Pour the whipped cream into a sterilized jar and store in the refrigerator for up to one month.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with migraine because of its calming and soothing properties. The essential oil of lavender, contained in these creams, has a natural ability to relax the mind and body, which can help alleviate tension headaches that often accompany migraines. Additionally, lavender's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce pain and swelling associated with migraines, providing relief from discomfort and distress. By applying lavender cream to affected areas, individuals may experience a reduction in migraine symptoms, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Lavender's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to migraine relief.
Anxiolytic and Sedative Properties: Lavender's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and sedative properties can help calm the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety triggers that can lead to migraines.

Pain-Relieving and Relaxing Properties: Lavender's analgesic (pain-relieving) and relaxing compounds, such as linalool, can help soothe headache pain and promote relaxation, which can also contribute to migraine relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in volatile oils, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate migraine pain.
Leaves: High in flavonoids, like apigenin and luteolin, which possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory activities, making them effective for soothing migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in late morning when fragrant, selecting 20 grams of stems with purple-blue flowers.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator or air drying method at room temperature for 2 weeks, monitoring moisture levels daily.
Combine dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 200 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a dark glass jar, allowing mixture to steep for 4 hours.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container, discarding solids and adjusting the mixture to a 1:3 ratio of plant material to carrier oil.
Whip the combined oil mixture with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler at 60°c for 15 minutes, resulting in a smooth, creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with migraine because they provide targeted relief to the scalp, where tension often builds up before a headache strikes. The cream's active ingredients, such as valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, work together to calm irritated nerves, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation of tense muscles. As the cream absorbs into the skin, it helps to quiet the mind and body, alleviating migraine triggers like stress and anxiety, and providing welcome relief from throbbing headaches.
remedy_properties: Sedative and Antispasmodic Properties: Valerian root has a sedative effect, which can help relax tense muscles and reduce muscle spasms in the neck and head, common causes of migraines.

Pain-Relieving and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Valerian root contains compounds that have been shown to inhibit inflammation and pain pathways, helping to reduce migraine pain intensity and duration.

GABA-Mimetic Properties: Valerian root has a GABA-mimicking effect, which can help increase the activity of the neurotransmitter GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, common triggers of migraines.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in soothing migraine pain.
Rhizomes: Containing volatile oils that have a sedative effect, helping to reduce frequency and severity of migraines.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry valeriana officinalis roots to ensure optimal potency for future use.
Grind dried valeriana officinalis roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 100 grams of powdered valeriana officinalis root with 200 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler for 20 minutes.
Heat the mixture to a temperature of 160°f and maintain it at that level for 1 hour stirring occasionally.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before transferring to glass containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with migraine because of its potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol in peppermint relaxes tense muscles, reduces pain and stiffness, and soothes the trigeminal nerve, which is often irritated during migraines. Additionally, peppermint's cooling effect can help constrict blood vessels and reduce inflammation in the brain, providing relief from throbbing headaches. As a natural and non-invasive treatment option, herbal peppermint creams offer a safe and effective way to alleviate migraine symptoms without relying on pharmaceuticals.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol, a primary compound found in peppermint oil, has natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to alleviate migraine pain by numbing the nerve endings and reducing inflammation.

Vasodilation: The menthol in peppermint oil also causes blood vessels to dilate, which can help to improve blood flow to the brain and reduce pressure, potentially alleviating migraine symptoms such as throbbing headaches and sensitivity to light and sound.

Gastrointestinal soothing: Peppermint oil has natural antispasmodic properties that can help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can exacerbate migraines by releasing tension-reducing neurotransmitters such as serotonin and endorphins.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other compounds, peppermint roots are excellent for relieving pain and inflammation associated with migraines.
Rhyzomes: The underground stems of peppermint plants contain a high concentration of menthone, which helps to relax muscles and alleviate tension headaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or purchase them from a reputable supplier at 30 grams per batch.
Dry the peppermint leaves in a dehydrator set at 60 degrees celsius for 24 hours or air dry them for 7 days.
Grind the dried peppermint leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder and sift through a mesh strainer to remove any lumps.
Mix the powdered peppermint with 100 grams of unsalted butter and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Pour the cooled cream mixture into sterile jars and store them in the refrigerator at 4 degrees celsius for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with migraine because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the mind and body. The herb's antispasmodic properties help to relax tense muscles and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to migraine pain. Additionally, lemon balm's calming effects on the nervous system may help to alleviate stress and anxiety, common triggers for migraines. By applying a topical cream containing lemon balm to the affected area, individuals may experience relief from migraine discomfort and frequency.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic effects: Lemon balm's antispasmodic properties can help relax tense muscles and reduce spasms, which is often a contributing factor to migraine pain.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activity: Lemon balm contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which are common underlying mechanisms of migraines.

Calming and sedative effects: The calming properties of lemon balm can help alleviate stress, anxiety, and irritability, all of which can trigger or exacerbate migraines, providing relief from their symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are often used due to their high concentration of essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate migraine pain.
Flowers: The flowers contain flavonoids and terpenes that have been shown to have analgesic and sedative effects, making them effective for calming migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried melissa officinalis herb and carrier oils such as sweet almond or coconut oil in equal proportions.
Combine the herbs and carrier oils in a clean glass jar and let it steep for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another container and discard the solids after 2-3 days of straining.
Add beeswax and vitamin e oil to the liquid mixture and melt the combination in a double boiler for 10-15 minutes.
Pour the melted mixture into glass containers and let it cool and solidify at room temperature or refrigerate for about 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood creams helps with migraine because they contain the natural compound thujone, which has a long history of use in traditional medicine to treat headache disorders. Thujone works by targeting the trigeminal nerve, which is responsible for transmitting pain signals to the brain, and has been shown to reduce inflammation and constrict blood vessels, both common contributors to migraine pain. By addressing these underlying mechanisms, wormwood creams may help alleviate migraine symptoms, providing relief from debilitating headaches and improving overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Wormwood contains anti-inflammatory compounds like sesquiterpenes, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in blood vessels, contributing to migraine relief.

Antispasmodic properties: The volatile oils in wormwood, such as absinthin and anabsinthol, can relax smooth muscle tissue and help alleviate migraines by reducing spasms and tension in blood vessels.
Pain-relieving compounds: Wormwood contains terpenoids like thujone, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help ease migraine pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing migraine pain.
Barks: Containing a high concentration of volatile oils, including camphor and thujone, which help to reduce inflammation and relax muscle tension associated with migraines.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of artemisia absinthium at dawn or dusk when essential oils are most concentrated.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation and low heat over 24 hours for optimal preservation.
Grind 20g of dried artemisia absinthium into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler set at medium heat stirring occasionally.
Combine the powdered artemisia absinthium with the melted wax-coconut oil mixture and stir for 10 minutes allowing it to cool and thicken.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marijuana creams helps with migraine because they provide targeted, localized relief to the affected area. The cream's active ingredients, such as cannabidiol (CBD) and tetrahydrocannabinol (THC), interact with the body's endocannabinoid system to reduce pain and inflammation. When applied topically, these herbal compounds can help alleviate migraine symptoms by relaxing muscles, reducing blood vessel constriction, and decreasing sensitivity to light and sound. This localized relief can be especially effective in treating migraines that are triggered or exacerbated by stress, fatigue, or environmental factors.
remedy_properties: Cannabidiol (CBD): CBD has potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which can help reduce migraine frequency and severity by targeting the underlying inflammation and pain pathways.

Terpenes: Terpenes, such as limonene and pinene, have been shown to have natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate migraine symptoms like pain, nausea, and sensitivity to light and sound.

Cannabigerol (CBG): CBG has been found to have a potent ability to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, both of which are thought to contribute to migraine development and progression.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of cannabidiol (CBD), which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making it effective in soothing migraine symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes have been found to contain high levels of myrcene, a terpene that can help alleviate tension and reduce inflammation associated with migraines.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 1 ounce of fresh cannabis sativa leaves and flowers for three days under indirect sunlight.
Blend the dried herb with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a heat-proof container.
Heat the mixture over low temperature for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts and the mixture cools to 140°f.
Strain the cream through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids, and let it cool completely at room temperature.
Store the cream in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to six months or freeze for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with migraine because they contain compounds that have a profound impact on the body's natural pain relief mechanisms. The cream's active ingredients, including maidenhair tree extract and other herbal components, work to reduce inflammation and relax tense muscles, which are common triggers of migraines. Additionally, these ingredients can help to calm the nervous system, reducing the frequency and severity of migraine episodes. As a result, using maidenhair tree creams may provide effective relief from migraine symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Ginkgo biloba extract may help reduce inflammation and vascular constriction that can contribute to migraine pain.
Vasodilatory properties: Ginkgo biloba extract may help dilate blood vessels, which could potentially help alleviate migraine-related headache pain by increasing blood flow to the brain.
Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba extract contains antioxidants that may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, both of which can contribute to migraines.
remedy_parts: Roots: Maidenhair tree roots have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce migraine pain and inflammation.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree contain antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help relax blood vessels and reduce migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality ginkgo biloba leaves and pure coconut oil, measuring 10g of dried leaves per 50ml oil.
Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves in a dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours before grinding into fine powder.
Mix the ground ginkgo biloba powder with 20% beeswax and 80% coconut oil to create a base mixture.
Heat the base mixture in a double boiler at 70°c for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until smooth and consistent.
Pour the warm cream into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and set before use within 7 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams helps with migraine because of its ability to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation. The cream's active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have a soothing effect on the skin and can help ease tension in the face and neck, common areas that can contribute to migraines. By applying the cream topically, it can also help to block pain signals from reaching the brain, providing rapid relief from migraine symptoms.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This active compound has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which can help reduce migraine symptoms by decreasing inflammation in blood vessels and reducing pain transmission.

Hypericin: This component has been shown to have a positive effect on the body's natural serotonin levels, which can help regulate migraine triggers and alleviate symptoms such as headache and sensitivity to light.

Flavonoids and phenolic acids: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, contributing to the development of migraines.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Containing hyperforin, a bioactive compound that has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making it effective in reducing migraine symptoms.
Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have analgesic and antispasmodic effects, helping to alleviate migraines by relaxing muscle spasms and reducing pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and 20 grams of organic coconut oil.
Grind the dried hypericum perforatum flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for five minutes.
Combine the ground hypericum perforatum powder with 100 milliliters of warm sweet almond oil and stir for one minute.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 65 degrees celsius for 10 minutes to facilitate infusion and decarboxylation.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a sterilized glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for two weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guelder rose creams helps with migraine because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream contains a unique blend of herbs, including guelder rose, willow bark, and lavender, which work together to reduce pain and swelling in the brain and scalp. As the cream is absorbed into the skin, it helps to relax tense muscles and calm the nervous system, providing rapid relief from migraine symptoms such as headache, nausea, and sensitivity to light and sound.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory effects: The cream's active compounds, such as rutin and quercetin, help to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation and pressure on the nerves.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Guelder Rose contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory activities, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the brain and surrounding tissues, providing relief from migraine discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: The cream's antioxidant compounds, including anthocyanins and phenolic acids, protect against oxidative stress and damage to nerve cells, which may contribute to migraine development and exacerbation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate migraine pain and discomfort.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can act as analgesics and antispasmodics to relieve migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh viburnum opulus leaves and flowers at dawn on a sunny day.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm ventilated area for 7-10 days.
Combine dried viburnum opulus leaves and flowers with 20 grams of beeswax and 40ml of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 30-40 minutes or until the wax has melted and mixed well with the herbal extract.
Pour the warm cream into sterilized jars and seal immediately, allowing it to cool before refrigerating at least 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with migraine because of its soothing and calming properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as apigenin and bisabolol, work by reducing inflammation and relaxing muscles in the face and scalp, which are common areas affected by migraines. Additionally, chamomile's natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties help to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with migraines, providing a sense of relief and relaxation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and irritation in blood vessels, which can contribute to migraine pain.

Antispasmodic properties: Chamomile's antispasmodic compounds relax smooth muscle tissue, including those responsible for blood vessel constriction, which can exacerbate migraine symptoms like headaches and sensitivity to light.
Anxiolytic (calming) properties: Chamomile's calming effects help reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers of migraines, by promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of the chamomile plant contain a high concentration of apigenin, an antioxidant that can help to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, providing relief from migraine symptoms.
Roots: Chamomile roots have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing pain and swelling associated with migraines.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 100g of distilled water and slowly heat it in a double boiler.
Add 20g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and allow the mixture to steep for 20 minutes.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container discarding solids.
Combine the infused liquid with 10g of beeswax and 5g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at a low temperature stirring occasionally until the wax has melted and the cream has emulsified.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with migraine because they contain flavonoids, particularly apigenin and luteolin, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and constrict blood vessels in the brain, providing relief from migraine symptoms such as pain, sensitivity to light and sound, and nausea. The creams also promote relaxation and calmness, reducing stress and anxiety that can trigger migraines.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as calyxin and flavonoids, can help reduce swelling and pain in blood vessels, which is a common migraine trigger.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold cream, including beta-carotene and lutein, may help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and migraine development.

Vasoconstrictive effects: The flavonoids in pot marigold cream have been shown to cause blood vessels to constrict, which may help reduce the inflammation and dilatation of blood vessels associated with migraines.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with migraines.
Barks: Containing bioactive compounds like flavonoids and terpenoids, barks possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry calendula officinalis flowers at dawn when their potency peaks to ensure maximum therapeutic benefits.
Combine dried calendula flowers with coconut oil in a double boiler setting, steeping for 30 minutes to extract beneficial compounds.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, squeezing out every last drop to retain essential oils and flavonoids.
Mix the extracted oil with beeswax, sweet almond oil, and a pinch of vitamin e in a small saucepan, heating gently until melted and well combined.
Pour the creamy mixture into sterilized jars, allowing it to cool and set at room temperature before use within 6 months for optimal potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with migraine because they contain bioactive compounds that target the root causes of the condition. The cream's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties help to reduce swelling in blood vessels, which can contribute to migraines. Additionally, dandelion's ability to support liver function and detoxification may help to eliminate toxins that can trigger migraines. By applying topically, the cream's active ingredients are able to directly interact with the affected area, providing fast-acting relief from migraine symptoms such as pain, sensitivity, and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce swelling and pain associated with migraines, providing relief from symptoms such as throbbing headaches and sensitivity to light and sound.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant compounds in dandelion cream may help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress, a known migraine trigger, thereby reducing the frequency and severity of migraines.

Analgesic and sedative: Dandelion's analgesic and sedative properties can help relieve pain and promote relaxation, which is often disrupted during a migraine episode, allowing for a more restful and headache-free state.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate migraine pain and reduce inflammation in the brain.
Leaves: The leaves are rich in antioxidants, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation that may contribute to migraines.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried dandelion root taraxacum officinale leaves in the early morning or evening when the essential oils are most concentrated.
Combine the 100g of dried herbs with 500ml of boiling water and allow it to steep for 20 minutes to extract the bioactive compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids then add 50g of beeswax and heat the mixture until melted.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for approximately 30 minutes before whipping it with an electric mixer until smooth and creamy texture is achieved.
Fill sterile jars with the cooled cream, seal tightly and store in a cool dark place for up to 6 months or refrigerate for up to 2 years.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Echinacea contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, potentially helping to alleviate migraine symptoms.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of Echinacea can help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage, which may contribute to the development and severity of migraines.

Analgesic and anti-pain compounds: Echinacea contains sesquiterpenes, such as alpha-eudesmol, which have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, potentially helping to reduce migraine pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to create a calming effect, which can help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation and stress.
Seeds: The seeds contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with migraines.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea angustifolia roots and wash them thoroughly with cool running water.
Chop the washed roots into small pieces and soak them in a mixture of 70% vodka and 30% distilled water for 48 hours.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids, then transfer the remaining liquid to a clean glass jar.
Combine the filtered liquid with 20% beeswax and 10% coconut oil in a double boiler, heating the mixture until it reaches a temperature of 120°f over the course of two hours.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken completely before transferring it to an airtight container and storing it in the refrigerator for up to six months.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with migraine because they contain Echinacea, a natural herb that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which is believed to contribute to migraines. Additionally, the cream's topical application allows for direct delivery of the active ingredients to the affected area, providing fast and targeted relief from pain and discomfort associated with migraine headaches. This natural alternative offers a gentle and effective way to alleviate migraine symptoms without resorting to pharmaceutical medications.

Licorice creams helps with migraine because they contain a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the skin, this compound can help reduce inflammation and spasms in blood vessels, common triggers of migraines. Additionally, licorice creams may also provide relief from pain and discomfort by stimulating the production of endorphins, natural painkillers produced by the body. As a result, many people find that using herbal licorice creams helps to alleviate migraine symptoms and restore their overall sense of well-being.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory compounds in the body, which can contribute to migraine symptoms.
Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants, neutralizing free radicals that can trigger or exacerbate migraines, and also help to improve blood flow to the brain, reducing the risk of migraines.
Saponins: Help to relax smooth muscles in the blood vessels, which can constrict during a migraine attack, thereby reducing pain and improving blood flow to the brain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in reducing migraine pain.
Rhyzomes: Contains isoflavones, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax blood vessels, helping to alleviate migraines.
Barks: Rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in reducing migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Sterilize glass jars and utensils by washing them in boiling water for 20 minutes.
Combine 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder with 200ml of distilled water, letting it steep for 8 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding the solids and preserving the liquid extract.
Mix the extracted liquid with 300g of coconut oil and 50g of beeswax, heating the mixture to 60°c while stirring until melted.
Pour the cooled cream into sterilized glass jars, labeling and storing them in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with migraine because of its unique ability to ease muscle tension and reduce inflammation. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to relax constricted blood vessels and reduce pain in the brain. Additionally, the soothing and warming sensation of the cream can provide rapid relief from migraine symptoms, helping to alleviate nausea and sensitivity to light and sound. As a result, herbal ginger creams offer a natural and effective way to manage migraines.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and brain, which can contribute to migraines.
Pain-relieving properties: Ginger's analgesic and anti-nociceptive properties help to numb the pain and reduce sensitivity to light and sound associated with migraines.
Vasodilatory effects: Ginger's vasodilatory properties help to dilate blood vessels, reducing constriction and pressure on blood vessels that can cause migraine headaches.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which help alleviate migraines by reducing pain and inflammation.
Roots: Contain natural pain-relievers and antioxidants that work together to soothe migraine symptoms and reduce frequency.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots measuring about 2-3 inches in length.
Wash the ginger roots thoroughly under cold running water and pat them dry with paper towels.
Grate the cleaned ginger roots into small pieces using a fine grater or food processor.
Mix the grated ginger with 10 tablespoons of coconut oil and let it infuse for at least 6 hours in an airtight container.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove the solids and discard the ginger solids then store the cream in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sesame creams helps with migraine because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds like sesamol and sesamin, which help to relax blood vessels and reduce inflammation in the brain. The creams also have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to alleviate tension headaches and migraines. Additionally, sesame oil has been shown to have analgesic and antispasmodic properties, further reducing pain and discomfort associated with migraines. Regular application of herbal sesame creams can provide long-lasting relief from migraine symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sesame cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like sesamin, sesamolin, and sesamol, which may help reduce inflammation in blood vessels and nerves, alleviating migraine pain.

Antioxidant activity: Sesame cream's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress, a known migraine trigger, thereby reducing the severity of migraines.

Vasodilatory effects: The sesamin content in sesame cream may also help relax blood vessels and improve circulation, which could help relieve the throbbing pain and pressure associated with migraines.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sesame roots are rich in sesamin and sesamolin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe migraine symptoms.
Seeds: Sesame seeds contain lignans, which have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with migraines.
remedy_recipe: Grind 2 tablespoons of dried sesamum indicum seeds into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the sesamum indicum powder with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes, stirring frequently, until the beeswax is fully melted and incorporated.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool slightly before adding 5 drops of lavender essential oil and mixing well.
Pour the cream into a small glass jar and store it in the refrigerator at 50°f to 60°f for up to 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with migraine because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and pain-relieving properties. The citral content in lemongrass has been shown to reduce inflammation and relax tense muscles, which are common triggers of migraines. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming effects help to alleviate stress and anxiety, two known contributors to migraine frequency and severity. By applying the cream topically, individuals can experience rapid relief from migraine symptoms, providing a natural and effective solution for managing these debilitating headaches.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemongrass cream's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress, inflammation, and cell damage, which can contribute to migraines by calming down blood vessels and reducing pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemongrass cream, such as citral and geraniol, help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing swelling and discomfort, and relieving pressure on blood vessels.

Pain-relieving and sedative properties: Lemongrass essential oil contains a compound called cineole, which has been shown to have analgesic and sedative effects, helping to relieve migraine pain, tension, and stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemongrass are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate migraine symptoms.

Rhyzomes: Lemongrass rhyzomes contain a high concentration of essential oils, particularly citral and geraniol, which have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in treating migraines.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh lemongrass leaves and dry them in the shade for 3-4 days to preserve potency.
Combine 10g of dried lemongrass leaves with 50ml of olive oil in a glass jar, steeping it for 6-8 hours under sunlight.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another container, squeezing out as much liquid as possible, retaining 30ml of herbal extract.
Mix 10g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil together in a double boiler, heating them until fully melted and well combined.
Combine the melted wax-oil mixture with 30ml of lemongrass extract, whipping it into a creamy consistency using an electric mixer for 2-3 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with migraine because of its unique ability to improve blood flow to the brain. The active compounds in rosemary, such as camphor and borneol, help to dilate blood vessels, increasing oxygenation and nutrient supply to the brain. This can help to alleviate migraines caused by vasospasms or reduced blood flow. Additionally, rosemary's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce inflammation and pain associated with migraines, providing relief from debilitating symptoms and improving overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compound carnosic acid: Carnosic acid helps reduce inflammation and swelling in blood vessels, which can contribute to migraine pain and frequency.
Antioxidant-rich flavonoids: Flavonoids in rosemary cream have been shown to neutralize free radicals that can damage brain cells and contribute to migraines, thereby reducing oxidative stress.

Cognitive-enhancing compound camphor: Camphor in rosemary cream has been found to improve blood circulation to the brain, which may help alleviate migraine symptoms by increasing oxygenation of the brain tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots have been used in traditional medicine to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, making them suitable for migraine relief.
Buds: Fresh rosemary buds are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe and calm the body during a migraine attack.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and rinse them with distilled water for 10 minutes to remove impurities.
Dry the rinsed leaves in a clean, well-ventilated area at room temperature for 24 hours to reduce moisture content.
Combine dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a double boiler and heat for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Allow the infused oil to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes before transferring it to airtight containers for storage.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for migraine are topical preparations that utilize natural ingredients to alleviate migraine symptoms. These creams work by providing a localized and targeted approach to pain relief, allowing for faster and more effective results. Examples of herbal creams that help with migraine include creams containing feverfew, ginger, and peppermint, which have been shown to reduce pain and inflammation. By using these creams, individuals can experience significant improvement in their quality of life, being able to resume daily activities without the burden of debilitating headaches.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat migraine with herbal creams is to apply topical preparations containing Feverfew, Butterbur, or Peppermint oil to the affected area. These herbs have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that help reduce migraine symptoms. Look for creams or ointments that combine these herbs with natural emollients like coconut or olive oil. Apply a small amount to the temples, forehead, or neck as needed to alleviate migraine pain and discomfort.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to migraine that are treated with herbal creams are tension headaches, cluster headaches, and sinusitis. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as arnica, peppermint, and eucalyptus help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation. These creams can be applied topically to the affected area, providing quick relief from discomfort and promoting relaxation.
